zh.Yaxley Hall, Yaxley, Suffolk, England: 英國英格蘭素福爾可亞可利
zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Agnes Fuller Countess Yaxley於1470年出生在英國素福爾可亞可利時，她的父親Thomas Clement Oldefylde26歲，母親Margaret Ward24歲。她於1489年與Sir John Sherman Earl Of Yaxley在英國素福爾可亞可利結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1528年在她的家鄉享年58歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭肯特貝泰沙朗聖 馬也斯 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Agnes Fuller Countess Yaxley于1470年出生在英国素福爾可亞可利时，她的父亲Thomas Clement Oldefylde26岁，母亲Margaret Ward24岁。她于1489年与Sir John Sherman Earl Of Yaxley在英国素福爾可亞可利结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1528年在她的家乡享年58岁，埋葬在英国英格兰肯特貝泰沙朗聖 馬也斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Fuller
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a dresser of cloth, from Middle English fuller ‘fuller of cloth’ (partly from Old English fullere, partly from Old French fouleor, foleur, Latin fullo). Raw cloth had to be fulled, i.e. scoured, cleansed, and thickened by beating or trampling it in water, a process also known as walking or tucking, hence the surnames Walker and Tucker alongside Fuller. These three terms and surnames are characteristic of different parts of England. In general, in Middle English, Fuller is southern and eastern, while Walker belongs to the west and north and Tucker is southwestern. Compare Fullen .
English: variant of Fullard with loss of -d.
German (Füller): occupational name for a person whose work involved filling, such as a dauber, or a nickname for a gourmand or glutton. Compare Filler .

variants.name: Walker
meanings: English (mainly North and Midlands) and Scottish: occupational name for a fuller, from Middle English walker, Old English wealcere (an agent derivative of wealcan ‘to walk, tread’), ‘one who trampled cloth in a bath of lye or kneaded it, in order to strengthen it’. This was the regular term for the occupation during the Middle Ages in western and northern England. Compare Fuller and Tucker . As a Scottish surname it has also been used as a translation of Gaelic Mac an Fhucadair ‘son of the fuller’. This surname is also very common among African Americans.

History: The name was brought to North America from northern England and Scotland independently by many different bearers in the 17th and 18th centuries. Samuel Walker came to Lynn, MA, c. 1630; Philip Walker was in Rehoboth, MA, in or before 1643. The surname was also established in VA before 1650; a Thomas Walker, born in 1715 in King and Queen County, VA, was a physician, soldier, and explorer.

variants.name: Filler
meanings: South German: occupational name for a flayer or skinner, from Middle High German viller ‘knacker’, from villen ‘to skin’.

variants.name: Fullen
meanings: English: occupational name for a fuller (see Fuller ), from Old French fulun ‘fuller’.
English (Lancashire): branch of this family in Lancashire probably represents a variant of Fulham (see Fullam ), reintroduced from Ireland.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Agnes Fuller Countess Yaxley was born in 1470, in Yaxley, Suffolk, England, her father, Thomas Clement Oldefylde, was 26 and her mother, Margaret Ward, was 24. She married Sir John Sherman Earl Of Yaxley in 1489, in Yaxley, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. She died in 1528, in her hometown, at the age of 58, and was buried in St Mary's Church, Betteshanger, Kent, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Agnes Fuller Countess Yaxley est née en 1470, à Yaxley, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Clement Oldefylde, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Margaret Ward, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John Sherman Earl Of Yaxley en 1489, à Yaxley, Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée en 1528, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 58 ans, et a été enterrée à St Mary's Church, Betteshanger, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Sie hat 1489 in Yaxley, Suffolk, England Sir John Sherman Earl Of Yaxley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb 1528 im Alter von 58 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in St Mary's Church, Betteshanger, Kent, England, United Kingdom begraben.

ko.St Mary's Church, Betteshanger, Kent, En: 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 벳섀그거 스트 마이'스 c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: Lady Agnes Fuller Countess Yaxley(이)가 1470년 영국 수폴크 야스리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Clement Oldefylde(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Ward(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1489년 영국 수폴크 야스리에서 Sir John Sherman Earl Of Yaxley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1528년 그녀의 고향에서 58세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 벳섀그거 스트 마이'스 c후르치에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Sir John Sherman Earl Of Yaxley em 1489, em Yaxley, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. ru.St Mary's Church, Betteshanger, Kent, En: ст мэри'с чурч, беттешангир, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Agnes Fuller Countess Yaxley родилась в 1470 г. в местности яксли, суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas Clement Oldefylde было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Ward, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John Sherman Earl Of Yaxley в 1489 г. в местности яксли, суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла в 1528 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 58 лет и была похоронена в местности ст мэри'с чурч, беттешангир, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания.
